mission.title: Install and Configure VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid
mission.description: VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid is a distribution that provides a robust platform for managing Kubernetes clusters in an enterprise environment. It leverages the Tanzu Framework and Carvel packaging to offer a cohesive experience for deploying and managing Kubernetes workloads. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with VMware's ecosystem.

steps.title: Install the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Tanzu CLI, which is necessary for managing Tanzu Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

curl -L https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/tanzu-framework/releases/download/v0.29.0/tanzu-framework-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/tanzu

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/tanzu

```

> Download the Tanzu CLI binary.
> Make the Tanzu CLI executable.

steps.title: Initialize the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step initializes the Tanzu CLI and sets up the necessary plugins.

```bash

tanzu init

```

> Initialize the Tanzu CLI environment.

steps.title: Create a Tanzu Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Tanzu Kubernetes cluster with specified parameters.

```bash

tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --num-nodes 3 --region us-west-2

```

> Create a cluster named 'my-cluster' with 3 nodes.
commands: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --num-nodes 3 --region us-west-2

steps.title: Configure kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to access the newly created cluster.

```bash

tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context

```

> Get the kubeconfig for the admin user.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' state.

uninstall.title: Delete the Tanzu Kubernetes cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the specified cluster and all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

tanzu cluster delete my-cluster

```

> Delete the cluster named 'my-cluster'.

uninstall.title: Remove the Tanzu CLI
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Tanzu CLI binary from your system.

```bash

rm /usr/local/bin/tanzu

```

> Remove the Tanzu CLI binary.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Tanzu CLI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your cluster configurations. This step upgrades the Tanzu CLI to the latest version.

```bash

curl -L https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/tanzu-framework/releases/download/v0.29.0/tanzu-framework-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/tanzu

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/tanzu

tanzu version

```

> Download the latest Tanzu CLI binary.
> Make the new Tanzu CLI executable.
> Verify the upgrade by checking the version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified node resources are not available in the selected region.

**Fix:**
Check the available resources in the specified region and adjust the node count or resource requests.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl CLI is not installed or not in the system PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using 'curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/$(curl -s https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl' and add it to your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig context for the cluster is not set correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context' to set the correct context.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'tanzu: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Tanzu CLI is not installed or not in the system PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Tanzu CLI is installed and the path '/usr/local/bin' is included in your PATH environment variable.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.29, improved CLI performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed deprecated commands related to cluster management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.4, ensure to update your cluster configurations to align with new command structures.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' state, confirming the cluster is operational.
codeSnippets: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --num-nodes 3 --region us-west-2

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
